notes:
Cycle 2: applied exactly three targeted fixes from code-quality-reviewer. No other behavior was changed.

Fix 1 (MEDIUM — Gamepad action/cancel edge-trigger, InputProvider.jsx):
  - Added `prevGpButtons` ref (line ~81) initialized to `{ a: false, b: false }`.
  - In the rAF tick (lines ~132-146), replaced the unconditional `actionPending.current = true` writes with rising-edge guards: `if (currentA && !prevGpButtons.current.a && !actionPending.current)` and the symmetric guard for GP_BTN_B / cancelPending.
  - `prevGpButtons.current` is updated to `{ a: currentA, b: currentB }` at the end of each frame when an active pad exists; reset to `{ a: false, b: false }` when no active pad is found (prevents phantom edge on reconnect).
  - The cycle-1 receipt claim 'Gamepad A button is also edge-triggered via the same pending mechanism' was INCORRECT. The pending mechanism drains the flag but does not prevent it being re-armed every frame while held. The fix above makes the claim accurate: the flag is now only armed on the rising edge (current=true, prev=false), exactly mirroring the !e.repeat keyboard guard.

Fix 2 (MEDIUM — Gate gamepad poll on connection state, InputProvider.jsx):
  - Added `connectedRef` (lines ~74-79) initialized from `navigator.getGamepads().some(Boolean)` at mount time so a pre-connected pad is detected immediately.
  - Added `onGamepadConnected` (sets `connectedRef.current = true`) and `onGamepadDisconnected` (re-checks `getGamepads().some(Boolean)` to handle multi-pad scenarios) listeners registered on `window` inside the single `useEffect` (lines ~257-264).
  - Both listeners are cleaned up in the existing cleanup return (lines ~272-273).
  - In the rAF tick (lines ~113-118), the `navigator.getGamepads()` call and the `Array.from(...).find(Boolean)` walk are now skipped entirely when `connectedRef.current` is false. The keyboard path runs unconditionally as required.

Fix 3 (LOW — Dead variable in TouchJoystick.jsx):
  - Removed `const touch = e.touches[0]` from `handleTouchStart` (was line ~61). The variable was declared but never read; the element rect (derived from `outerRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()`) is what the handler actually uses to set the joystick origin.
